tools.name: ruff-check
tools.notes: Configured in pyproject.toml but NOT enforced in CI - only format checking is gating

linting.notes: CI only enforces ruff format checking, not ruff check (linting). Pre-commit hooks configured for ruff-format and nbstripout.

testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures and papermill for notebook execution

notebook_parameters.description: Validates notebooks have required 'parameters' cell tag for papermill

notebook_execution.description: Executes notebooks with papermill to verify they run without errors
notebook_execution.infrastructure: GPU, CUDA 12.4, large ML dependencies (docling, transformers, milvus)
notebook_execution.notes: Requires GPU and heavy dependencies - runs on AWS EC2 g6e.xlarge in CI

testing.notes: Notebook parameter validation works in standard container. Notebook execution requires GPU infrastructure and ML dependencies. Test files use pathlib and papermill for execution.

gating_checks.name: Compile KFP pipelines
gating_checks.notes: Ensures pipeline code changes are accompanied by regenerated compiled YAML

gating_checks.name: Test Local Pipeline Runners
gating_checks.notes: Only runs on upstream repo (opendatahub-io/data-processing), not forks

advisory_checks.name: Execute All Notebooks
advisory_checks.notes: Full notebook execution post-merge, requires GPU and CUDA 12.4

ci.notes: Most checks are lightweight and run on ubuntu-latest. GPU-intensive checks (notebook execution, KFP local runners) require AWS EC2 instances and only run on the upstream repository. Forks get a PR check notification but GPU tests are skipped.

build.notes: No build step for notebooks. KFP pipeline compilation requires installing heavy dependencies (docling, tesserocr). Notebook execution requires GPU-specific packages.

conventions.test_naming: pytest convention - test_* functions, test_* files
conventions.import_style: Standard Python imports, ruff configured for import sorting (I001)
conventions.notebook_requirements: All notebooks must have a code cell tagged with 'parameters' for papermill execution
conventions.notes: Notebooks are organized into use-cases/ and tutorials/. Python code is in kubeflow-pipelines/ subdirectories.

lint_commands.notes: 6 notebooks checked, all passed nbstripout verification
Found 3 lint violations (I001, UP015, UP017) - but this is NOT enforced in CI

test_commands.notes: 7 tests passed in 1.02s - validates notebooks have parameters cells
Requires GPU, CUDA, and heavy ML dependencies (docling, transformers, milvus) - cannot validate in standard container

container_recipe.notes: Standard Python container sufficient for format/lint checks and parameter validation. Notebook execution and KFP compilation require GPU infrastructure and specialized dependencies.

results.notes: Ruff linting finds violations but is NOT enforced in CI - only format checking is gating

validation.summary: Install OK, format checks OK (Python + notebooks), parameter tests OK (7 passed). Notebook execution tests not run (require GPU/ML dependencies). KFP compilation not tested (requires docling, tesserocr).

gaps: Notebook execution tests require GPU (CUDA 12.4) and heavy ML dependencies (docling, transformers, milvus) - cannot be validated in standard container
KFP pipeline compilation requires system dependencies (tesserocr needs libtesseract) and heavy ML libraries - not tested in validation
Ruff linting (ruff check) is configured but not enforced in CI - only format checking runs
No unit tests for Python code in kubeflow-pipelines/ - only notebook validation
Full integration testing requires AWS infrastructure (EC2 GPU instances, secrets) - only available to upstream repo
Forks cannot run GPU-dependent CI checks - they only get basic validation
agent_readiness_explanation: Format checking and notebook structure validation work perfectly in a standard Python 3.12 container. An agent can validate formatting (ruff, nbstripout) and notebook parameters with 100% reliability. However, actual notebook execution and KFP pipeline testing require GPU infrastructure and heavy ML dependencies (docling, transformers, milvus) that are only available in specialized CI environments. An agent can do partial validation (format + structure) but not full execution testing.
